payload.text:
Toxicity Detector

AI-Powered Multi-Language Game Chat Analysis

Chat Input

Processed text with highlighted triggers will appear here...

Analysis Dashboard

0%
SAFE
Detected Lang: -
  • Hate Speech
  • Harassment
  • Profanity
  • Threat
  • Racism
  • Bullying
  • Sexual Content
  • Spamming

Analysis History (Last 5)

**Developing React Integration**

I'm now focused on integrating React with the existing static HTML/JS template. The goal is to build a modern, interactive front-end for the Game Chat Toxicity Detection System, drawing on Google's Gemini API. My approach emphasizes a Cyberpunk/Futuristic visual style. I'm exploring how to translate the existing structure into React components, ensuring a smooth transition.


**Defining App Structure**

I've got the app components mapped out. I'm building a multi-language toxicity detection system. My focus is the `gemini-3-flash-preview` for quick and effective classification using a system instruction as a moderator. The JSON output will be key. I'm leaning heavily on `index.tsx`, `App.tsx`, `index.html`, and a suite of TypeScript components like `Gauge.tsx`, `ResultDashboard.tsx`, and `ChatInput.tsx`. I'll also add a `StarBackground.tsx` for visual flair and  `geminiService.ts`.


**Refining Component Interactions**

I'm now integrating crucial components. I've defined `constants.ts` for styling. `index.html` uses Tailwind and Google Fonts. In `App.tsx`, I'm handling state and layout. `geminiService.ts` focuses on constructing and sending prompts to Gemini. I've designed the system prompt to receive a detailed JSON, including scores, levels, categories, and highlights. I'm also converting the star background logic into a React `useEffect` with a `canvas` ref, for a smooth integration of the star background.

entries.description: App metadata and configuration for the Toxicity Detector system.
Main HTML entry point with Tailwind CSS and font links.
Global TypeScript interfaces for the application.
Constants and category data for the UI.
